mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate directly influences equity discount rates; higher interest rates reduce the present value of future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns is between -0.03 and +0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the negative direction is not statistically significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When monthly housing starts fall below 1000 thousand units, it signals a severe contraction in residential investment that propagates to consumer spending and banking sector losses, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After the trigger activates, the proportion of positive 60-day forward returns exceeds 50% in at least 10 out of 15 subsequent trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: The real short-term rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation) determines the relative attractiveness of equities versus cash; a negative real rate drives investors out of cash into stocks, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The spread between FEDFUNDS and CPI inflation fails to produce a monotonic relationship with forward returns: rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During a recessionary environment where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, a further month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls indicates deepening labor market distress and falling aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 5.5%, PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 20-day returns in at least 40% of instances, or the average return following declines is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising level of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and economic strength, which lifts corporate earnings and investor sentiment, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month irrespective of other factors.
failure_condition: The proportion of positive 20-day forward returns following month-over-month increases in INDPRO is below 55% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When real retail sales (RSAFS) experience a month-over-month decline, it signals an abrupt consumer spending pullback that immediately pressures corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: After negative RSAFS growth, positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 45% of instances, or the average 5-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is lower than a simple Taylor rule benchmark (using UNRATE for slack and CPIAUCSL for inflation), monetary policy is accommodative, stimulating investment and consumption, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The deviation from the Taylor rule benchmark fails to predict 60-day forward returns: directional hit rate below 55% or rank IC absolute value below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During the late-cycle expansion phase when industrial production growth is positive but decelerating, a sudden decline in housing starts acts as an early warning of recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth < 1% and HOUST month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: When the trigger conditions are met, the average 20-day forward return is not negative, or positive returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances over the walk-forward period.
